word_list.pronunciation: ˈaɪdəl fˈoʊk ɔːɹ pˈiːpəl hæv ðə lˈiːst lˈiːʒɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that people who waste their time and are not productive tend to have less free time to pursue their interests and enjoy leisure activities

examples.x: The procrastinator complained about not having enough time to pursue their hobbies, but idle folk have the least leisure, and their lack of productivity left them with little free time.

word_list.word: there will be sleeping enough in the grave
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ wɪl biː slˈiːpɪŋ ɪnˈʌf ɪnðə ɡɹˈeɪv

senses.definition: used to emphasize that one should not waste their life by sleeping too much or being idle, but instead make the most of their time on earth

examples.x: I don't like to waste my weekends sleeping because there will be sleeping enough in the grave, and I want to enjoy my time while I'm alive.

word_list.word: a lazy sheep thinks its wool heavy
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ lˈeɪzi ʃˈiːp θˈɪŋks ɪts wˈʊl hˈɛvi

senses.definition: used to suggest that lazy or unmotivated individuals tend to perceive tasks as more difficult than they actually are, making even small responsibilities seem burdensome

word_list.word: an idle brain is the devil's workshop
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈaɪdəl bɹˈeɪn ɪz ðə dˈɛvəlz wˈɜːkʃɑːp

senses.definition: used to imply that when a person has nothing to do or is not engaged in productive activity, they are more likely to engage in mischief, immoral behavior, or harmful actions

word_list.word: as good be an addled egg as an idle bird
word_list.pronunciation: æz ɡˈʊd biː ɐn ˈædəld ˈɛɡ æz ɐn ˈaɪdəl bˈɜːd

senses.definition: used to suggest that idleness and lack of effort are to be avoided, as they lead to wastefulness and unfulfillment

word_list.word: by doing nothing we learn to do ill
word_list.pronunciation: baɪ dˌuːɪŋ nˈʌθɪŋ wiː lˈɜːn tə dˈuː ˈɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that idleness and lack of effort can lead to developing bad habits or skills

word_list.word: the devil finds work for idle hands (to do|)
word_list.pronunciation: ðə dˈɛvəl fˈaɪndz wˈɜːk fɔːɹ ˈaɪdəl hˈændz tə dˈuː

senses.definition: used to imply that when people are not engaged in useful work, they are more likely to get into trouble or engage in harmful activities

word_list.word: idle hands are the devil's playthings
word_list.pronunciation: ˈaɪdəl hˈændz ɑːɹ ðə dˈɛvəlz plˈeɪθɪŋz

senses.definition: used to suggest that when people are not busy or occupied with useful work, they are more likely to engage in mischief or harmful activities

word_list.word: the worst wheel of the cart creaks most
word_list.pronunciation: ðə wˈɜːst wˈiːl ʌvðə kˈɑːɹt kɹˈiːks mˈoʊst

senses.definition: used to imply that the weakest or least capable member of a group often complains the most or causes the most problems

word_list.word: the tongue of the idle person is never idle
word_list.pronunciation: ðə tˈʌŋ ʌvðɪ ˈaɪdəl pˈɜːsən ɪz nˈɛvɚɹ ˈaɪdəl

senses.definition: used to suggest that idle people tend to talk a lot and engage in idle chatter, often without thinking about the consequences of their words or the impact that they might have on others

word_list.word: idleness is the root of (all|) evil
word_list.pronunciation: ˈaɪdəlnəs ɪz ðə ɹˈuːt ʌv ˈɔːl ˈiːvəl

senses.definition: used to imply that when people have nothing to do and no purpose, they are more likely to engage in negative or harmful activities
used to suggest that when people are idle or inactive, they are unlikely to achieve anything positive or worthwhile
